seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient is being evaluated for kidney stones on referral from his primary care provider.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: About a week ago the patient developed sudden onset right back pain while working in a barn moving hay.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: At the time of onset of right back pain the patient broke out into a sweat.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: At the time of onset of right back pain the patient became very nauseated.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: After developing symptoms the patient saw his doctor, who ordered a CT scan.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The prior CT scan ordered by the patient's doctor showed a kidney stone.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient reports never having had a kidney stone before this episode.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient's father has had kidney stones in the past.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: During the episode the patient's right back pain radiated almost down to the groin.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: Since the initial episode the patient has not had more severe pain, with discomfort localized to the right back area.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: After learning he had a kidney stone, the patient had recurrent episodes of pain a couple of times, each of shorter duration.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient has been straining his urine using a provided cup.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient has not seen any stone material in the urine strainer.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient has not noticed darker than usual urine.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: patient

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient's vital signs are normal without fever.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The patient's blood pressure and heart rate are normal.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: On abdominal exam there is no tenderness to palpation.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: On abdominal exam there is no rebound or guarding.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: There is right costovertebral angle tenderness on exam.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician reviewed the CT results for this visit.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: CT shows a 0.5 cm stone in the proximal right ureter.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: CT shows no evidence of hydronephrosis.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician assesses that the patient currently has a right ureteral kidney stone.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician attributes the right costovertebral angle tenderness to inflammation from the kidney stone.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician recommends the patient increase fluid intake to facilitate urination and passing the stone.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician will prescribe oxycodone 5 mg every 6 to 8 hours as needed for pain.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician advises the patient to continue taking Tylenol between oxycodone doses for breakthrough pain.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician advises the patient to continue using the urine strainer until the stone passes.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician will order a basic metabolic panel.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician will order a urinalysis.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician will order a urine culture.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The purpose of the urinalysis is to determine if antibiotics are needed for a possible infection.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: The clinician plans to see the patient back in about one to two weeks.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: If the stone has not passed in one to two weeks, the clinician plans to discuss further treatment options.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician

seed_gold_caps.fact_text: If the stone has not passed, the clinician may consider lithotripsy to break up the stone.
seed_gold_caps.speaker: clinician
